title: Refactor _patch_stage into shared mixin and complete meta-test coverage
analysis:
The BaseStageValidationTest base class extraction (task item 1) is already complete — both TestProjectIdValidation and TestRunIdValidation inherit from it at lines 437 and 614 respectively. Three items remain:

(a) _MOCK_TYPES constant: The tuple (AsyncMock, MagicMock) appears 3 times (lines 587, 595, 611) in meta-tests inside TestProjectIdValidation. Extract to a module-level constant.

(b) Postconditions on test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect (line 599): Currently tests that custom cli_side_effect is wired and assemble_payload is patched, but does NOT save originals before the `with` block or assert restoration after exit. The sibling test (line 578) does this correctly — mirror that pattern.

(c) Exception-safety meta-test: No test currently verifies that _patch_stage restores originals when an exception is raised inside the `with` block. Add test_patch_stage_restores_on_exception.

All changes are confined to a single test file. No production code changes required.

steps.description: Write test_mock_types_constant_defined in TestBaseStageValidationContract that asserts a module-level _MOCK_TYPES constant exists in test_stages, is a tuple, and contains both AsyncMock and MagicMock. This test will fail because _MOCK_TYPES is not yet defined.

Define _MOCK_TYPES = (AsyncMock, MagicMock) as a module-level constant near the imports (after line 33). Replace all 3 inline (AsyncMock, MagicMock) tuples (lines 587, 595, 611) with _MOCK_TYPES. Run tests to verify step-1 test passes and existing meta-tests still pass.

Enhance test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect (line 599) with postcondition assertions: save original_run_stage_via_cli and original_assemble_payload before the `with` block, then after exit assert (a) base_module.run_stage_via_cli is original_run_stage_via_cli, (b) stage.assemble_payload is not an instance of _MOCK_TYPES, (c) stage.assemble_payload == original_assemble_payload. This mirrors the pattern in test_patch_stage_patches_assemble_payload_and_run_stage. This tests existing working behavior so should pass immediately.

Write test_patch_stage_restores_on_exception in TestProjectIdValidation. The test: (1) creates a stage, (2) saves original_run_stage_via_cli and original_assemble_payload, (3) enters self._patch_stage(stage) in a try/except, (4) raises RuntimeError('boom') inside the with block, (5) catches the exception outside, (6) asserts base_module.run_stage_via_cli is original_run_stage_via_cli, (7) asserts stage.assemble_payload is not an instance of _MOCK_TYPES, (8) asserts stage.assemble_payload == original_assemble_payload. This verifies context manager cleanup on abnormal exit and should pass immediately.

design_decisions.decision: Keep meta-tests in TestProjectIdValidation rather than moving to a separate class
design_decisions.rationale: The existing meta-tests (test_patch_stage_patches_assemble_payload_and_run_stage, test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect) already live in TestProjectIdValidation. Since the mixin is shared, these tests exercise _patch_stage for both inheriting classes. Moving them would be churn with no coverage benefit.

design_decisions.decision: Place _MOCK_TYPES constant at module level rather than on BaseStageValidationTest
design_decisions.rationale: Module-level constants are the idiomatic Python pattern for reusable type tuples. Putting it on the base class would require self._MOCK_TYPES or cls._MOCK_TYPES references which add noise. The constant is used only in meta-tests within this module.

design_decisions.decision: Add _MOCK_TYPES test to TestBaseStageValidationContract rather than creating a new class
design_decisions.rationale: TestBaseStageValidationContract already verifies the structural contract of the shared test infrastructure (base class members). A module-level constant supporting that infrastructure fits naturally in this class.

design_decisions.decision: No separate impl steps for postcondition and exception-safety meta-tests
design_decisions.rationale: These tests verify existing context manager behavior that already works correctly. There is no production code to implement — the tests themselves are the deliverable. Both should pass immediately upon creation.

reuse.how: All new meta-tests exercise this existing context manager — no need to create new patch infrastructure
Copy the save-originals-before / assert-restored-after pattern into test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect and the new exception-safety test
Reuse existing module-level import to access run_stage_via_cli for identity checks in meta-tests
